implementationGroups.title: Source behavior: com/example
requiredBehaviors: The source app uses Android `Application` bootstrap through `FoodikeApp : Application()` plus Hilt application registration.
`FoodikeApp` is only an `Application` entry annotation surface; the source file does not request permissions or touch protected APIs.
riskHints: critical/high behavior cannot close on build pass alone
related platform capability must be resolved with Cangjie-callable API, replacement, fallback blocker, or deferred evidence

implementationGroups.title: Source behavior: data/data_source
requiredBehaviors: `FakeData.kt` only defines in-memory sample menus, restaurants, ads, favourites, and cart rows; it does not implement file or storage behavior.
`FakeData.kt` references drawable-backed sample images only; it does not implement bitmap decoding, EXIF handling, zoom, preview, or gallery logic.
`FakeData.kt` itself does not carry intent, URI, bundle, or deeplink behavior; it only supplies seeded content later consumed by screen flows.
`FakeData.kt` contains no toolbar, drawer, menu, tab, or route-command code; it only seeds lists consumed by navigation-aware screens.
`FakeData.kt` contains no permission request or protected API behavior.
riskHints: critical/high behavior cannot close on build pass alone
broad shell endpoint risk
related platform capability must be resolved with Cangjie-callable API, replacement, fallback blocker, or deferred evidence
acceptanceEvidence: Closed critical/high features must record sourceBehavior, targetBehavior, parityLevel, targetEndpoints, and verificationEvidence.
Build pass alone is not per-feature verification.

Replace root/shell/whole-file endpoints with precise target functions, types, resource files, or tests before closure.

implementationGroups.title: Source behavior: data/repository
requiredBehaviors: `getAds()` immediately returns the fake advertisement list.
`getFoodItems()` immediately returns the fake recommended food/category list.
`getRestaurantFromName(name)` performs an exact-name lookup over the fake restaurant list.
`getRestaurants()` immediately returns the full fake `restaurantList` from `FakeData.kt`.
`HomeRepositoryImpl` only returns fake restaurant, ad, and food lists and does not touch permission-protected APIs.
`toggleLoginState()` flips the persisted login boolean that controls startup and authenticated navigation.
The source exposes login state as a `Flow<Boolean>` backed by DataStore updates.
`LoginRepositoryImpl` only reads and toggles a boolean DataStore preference; it does not request permissions or call protected APIs.
`LoginRepositoryImpl` stores the login toggle in a single boolean preference file/key (`user_login_state_pref` / `user_login_state`).
`getCartItems()` emits the seeded cart items held by the shared repository.
riskHints: critical/high behavior cannot close on build pass alone
broad shell endpoint risk
related platform capability must be resolved with Cangjie-callable API, replacement, fallback blocker, or deferred evidence
acceptanceEvidence: Closed critical/high features must record sourceBehavior, targetBehavior, parityLevel, targetEndpoints, and verificationEvidence.

visualEvidenceRequired: verify safe actions show visible nearby before/after state feedback
settings/preference rows should show current value/toggle state, not only a generic Open button
contractScopeNotes: uiContract is derived from static Android analysis and is a planning aid, not a complete UI specification.
Treat listed screens, routes, actions, state feedback, and visual evidence as the minimum known UI evidence.
If source-visible UI behavior is absent from uiContract, preserve it and update matrix/checklist evidence instead of dropping it.
sourceReviewRequired: re-read relevant source files before implementing or closing UI-related rows
re-read layouts, menus, preference XML, drawable/theme resources, adapters, navigation, and back-stack code when present
check runtime state, permission/result callbacks, data refresh, and item-action flows that static analysis may miss

implementationGroups.title: Source behavior: di
requiredBehaviors: `AppModule.kt` relies on Hilt singleton component lifecycle and application-context injection to provide repositories.
`provideLoginRepository()` makes the session repository available as a singleton dependency for the rest of the source app.
`AppModule.kt` only provides repository bindings and does not request permissions or configure protected API access.

matchedGroups.title: Startup routing and splash gate
requiredBehaviors: Home start when login state is true.
Onboarding start when login state is false.
Loading gate clears only after start destination is resolved.
riskHints: Source uses blocking Flow read in startup; target should preserve user-visible gating without unnecessary shell coupling.
acceptanceEvidence: build gate pass
focused startup session test pass or compile pass
uiContracts: startup route chosen before normal navigation shell

matchedGroups.title: Session and selection repositories
requiredBehaviors: Repository reads provide ads, food, and restaurants for home UI.
Selected restaurant write must be observable by detail/history/cart flows.
Session toggle persists across startup.
riskHints: Source fake repositories are simple but UI relies on their immediate emission semantics.
acceptanceEvidence: focused repository refresh tests
build gate pass

phaseGroupIndex.title: Platform capability: Background work, services, receivers, widgets, and notifications
Platform capability: Runtime permissions and protected platform access

coordinationNotes: Prefer extending existing route/navigation/state surfaces over creating parallel shells.
If a shared surface must change, keep the change minimal and update only evidence rows tied to this group.
UI groups should preserve route exclusivity, back behavior, visible action feedback, and page-specific builders.
Repository/store groups should preserve existing data ownership and avoid duplicating global state.
